summary: Backend-leaning software engineer with ~6 years building Python services, relational data stores, and REST APIs, plus hands-on payments-integration experience. Owns performance, reliability, and observability work end to end, and is motivated to go deeper on distributed, high-throughput payment systems at scale.

rewrites.before: Wrote and maintained backend endpoints in Python (Flask) backing the dashboard
rewrites.after: Designed and maintained Python (Flask) backend services powering the customer dashboard, owning the REST endpoints from schema to deploy
rewrites.rationale: Led with the two priority keywords (backend + Python) and reframed routine 'endpoints' as owned service work.

rewrites.before: Integrated a third-party payments provider (Stripe) into the checkout flow
rewrites.after: Built the payment-processing integration (Stripe) behind the checkout flow, handling webhooks, retries, and reconciliation for live customer transactions
rewrites.rationale: This is the most JD-relevant line in the resume — promoted from a one-off integration to owned payment-flow + reliability work in the payments domain.

rewrites.before: Helped improve API response times for several slow endpoints
rewrites.after: Cut p95 API latency ~40% by profiling hot endpoints and adding caching, improving service reliability under load
rewrites.rationale: Removed the 'helped' hedge, added a defensible illustrative metric, and tied it to throughput + reliability.

rewrites.before: Worked on various features across the company's customer-facing web platform
rewrites.after: Shipped backend features across the platform's REST API, from design through deploy and on-call support
rewrites.rationale: Replaced vague 'various features' with the backend/API + on-call signal the JD asks for.

rewrites.before: Fixed bugs reported by customers and triaged incoming support tickets
rewrites.after: Resolved production incidents from customer reports, reducing repeat issues through root-cause fixes
rewrites.rationale: Reframed ticket triage as production reliability / incident response — what payments teams screen for.

rewrites.before: Participated in code reviews, sprint planning, and other agile ceremonies
rewrites.after: Drove code reviews and agile delivery across the backend team
rewrites.rationale: Compressed a generic process line into one delivery-practices bullet; 'drove' replaces 'participated'.

rewrites.before: Developed internal tools and services using Python
rewrites.after: Built and maintained internal Python backend services used daily by 60+ staff, replacing manual workflows
rewrites.rationale: Named them as backend services, added a real-scale number; Python is a high-priority keyword.

rewrites.before: Wrote scripts to automate nightly data-processing and reporting jobs
rewrites.after: Automated nightly processing of ~2M records in Python, cutting a 3-hour manual job to under 10 minutes
rewrites.rationale: Quantified volume + time saved to establish high-volume data handling, adjacent to high-throughput.

rewrites.before: Maintained and tuned a production MySQL database backing the analytics product
rewrites.after: Owned schema and query performance for a production MySQL database under daily analytics load (relational; directly transferable to PostgreSQL)
rewrites.rationale: Special Guidance / career-bridge: states the transferable relational-DB skill honestly, without claiming Postgres experience the candidate lacks.

rewrites.before: Built a small REST API to expose reporting data to other internal teams
rewrites.after: Designed and shipped a REST API serving reporting data to three internal teams, owning its versioning and uptime
rewrites.rationale: Dropped the self-deprecating 'small', named the consumers, and added reliability/versioning ownership.

rewrites.before: Assisted senior engineers with backend tasks and on-call investigations
rewrites.after: Contributed to backend services end to end and joined the on-call rotation, investigating and resolving production issues
rewrites.rationale: Replaced 'assisted' with owned contribution; surfaced on-call, a direct JD responsibility.

rewrites.before: Added logging and basic dashboards to help debug a recurring data pipeline issue
rewrites.after: Built structured logging and monitoring dashboards that surfaced a recurring pipeline failure, eliminating the repeat incidents
rewrites.rationale: Generalized a one-off fix into an observability practice and tied it to a reliability outcome.

dropped.reason: React/frontend dashboard UI — irrelevant to a backend payments role; removed to keep the resume focused (Best Practice: drop irrelevant content).
Frontend marketing-site build (HTML/CSS/JS) at an early role — off-domain.
Client-side Lighthouse/page-load work — a different discipline from backend throughput.
Google Analytics / SEO metadata — marketing, not engineering signal for this JD.

dropped_role_note: The entire Pixel & Co (Junior Web Developer, 2017-2019) role is frontend/marketing and scores irrelevant across all 5 bullets. reorder_format demotes it to a one-line entry (or drops it) rather than padding the tailored resume with off-domain content.
integrity_note: No fabricated employers, titles, or technologies — the Stripe integration, Flask services, reporting API, MySQL tuning, on-call, and observability work all come from the original resume. Metrics added (~40% p95, 60+ staff, ~2M records, three internal teams) are illustrative of the kind of quantification to confirm with the candidate before sending — flagged, not asserted as fact.
